title: How to Certify a Uniface Application
breadcrumbs: How to Certify a Uniface Application
text: How to Certify a Uniface Application Procedure to certify a Uniface application. Before you Begin Always store your private key(s) in a safe place, like a password manager. Never certify or deploy development tools like IDE resources or ide.uar . You can certify and deploy usys.uar if your application depends on it. Steps to Certifying a Uniface Application Certifying a Uniface application involves three key steps that take place during different phases of the application lifecycle: Generate Private/Public Key Pair: Begin by creating a pair of cryptographic keys—one private and one public. These keys will be used to secure and certify the application. Certify the Runtime Resources (During Packaging): During the packaging phase, use the private key to sign the runtime resources. This certification step ensures that the resources are authentic and have not been tampered with, validating them for secure execution in the Uniface environment. Encrypt the Assignment File Paths (At Deployment): At the deployment, encrypt the public key and the paths in the assignment. This adds an additional layer of security, ensuring that these file paths are protected from unauthorized access or modification. Procedure Generate Keys Create a private/public key pair with cert.exe or OpenSSL using the following command(s): Note: If you already have a key pair, you can skip this step. cert.exe -key OR Explain this code openssl genpkey -out private.pem -algorithm RSA -pkeyopt rsa_keygen_bits:2048 openssl pkey -in private.pem -pubout -out public.pem Copy Note: openssl command syntax is subject to change, refer to OpenSSL's manual for the latest syntax. Application Packaging - Certify the Uniface resources You need to certify all its resources to authorize a Uniface application to run with encrypted paths. The following are some examples of resource files being certified (authorized) to run against encrypted paths: Explain this code cert.exe -certify "C:\<path>\project\resources" "C:\<path>\private.pem" cert.exe -certify "C:\<path>\deploy.uar" "C:\<path>\private.pem" cert.exe -certify "C:\<path>\usys.uar" "C:\<path>\private.pem" Copy Deploying the Application Create a [ENCRYPTED_PATHS] section Gather all your sensitive paths and store them under the ENCRYPTED_PATHS section as shown below: Explain this code [ENCRYPTED_PATHS] ; Path to the SQLite database for the Uniface Repository: $DBMS SLE:.\dbms\usys.db ; Path to the SQLite database for User defined data: $DBMS_DEF SLE:.\dbms\userdata.db $SYS $DBMS $UUU $DBMS $IDF $DBMS $DEF $DBMS_DEF $REMOTE_C TCP:1.1.1.1+13001|UnifaceServer|Uniface_Server123|test /ex Copy Encrypt your new section with the Pathscrambler Run the pathscrambler with the -encrypt option to encrypt all your sensitive paths using the following command: pathscrambler.exe -encrypt "C:\<path>\deploy.asn" "C:\<path>\public.pem" The pathscrambler will generate a new file with the .enc file extension. Inside the file, you expect to see a very long string as shown below: Explain this code [ENCRYPTED_PATHS] (!AupzLPMF+FBL/0gD1CJ/3DrvKpU/2kRHEpI8kCjH2yDhNvaAB/FDODMkZgGk6!)((AuSBG7Q6C/uio3VTxg=)) Copy You can now use the ENCRYPTED_PATHS section instead of PATHS in your deployment ASN file. Related Links cert.exe The cert.exe executable is used to certify Uniface applications and facilitates the creation of public/private key pairs. pathscrambler.exe The pathscrambler.exe executable is used to protect sensitive information such as paths, connection strings, logon information and logicals in Uniface assignment files, Java Servlet settings, and the ProcScript open command.
